title: Recall a prior architecture decision and rationale
purpose: Demonstrate that a later workflow can recover why the project is runtime-first and why the OpenCode adapter must remain thin.

seedMemory.text: The core product is a local-first persistent memory runtime for coding agents, not an OpenCode plugin-first product.
Keep the OpenCode adapter thin so host integration details do not drive runtime design or block core verification.

task.prompt: Explain the current system boundary and why the adapter should stay thin while planning the next implementation milestone.

expectedRecall: The core product is a local-first persistent memory runtime for coding agents, not an OpenCode plugin-first product.
Keep the OpenCode adapter thin so host integration details do not drive runtime design or block core verification.
successCriteria: The memory-assisted path surfaces both the architecture boundary and its rationale.
The later workflow preserves runtime-first planning instead of drifting into plugin-first scope.
The recalled context stays explainable and bounded before injection.
